title: Atropis canbyi
taxonomy: Atropis canbyi is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. It is a perennial plant with a rosette of leaves and produces small white flowers in clusters. Atropis canbyi is sometimes classified as a synonym of Atropis gilliatensis, but its exact classification may vary depending on the source. The plant is typically found in dry areas with rocky outcroppings, where it can thrive in full sun. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this species within the Apiaceae family.

intro: Atropis canbyi, also known as Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the western United States, characterized by its clusters of red berries and compound leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential antiviral and antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Atropis canbyi include phenolic compounds such as ellagic acid and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
